name: Margarita
preparation: Rim the coupe glass with salt.
Strain into a chilled coupe glass.

movieAppearances.note: Tom Cruise's bartender character makes and serves Margaritas in several scenes.
Antonio Banderas drinks in a Mexican bar where Margaritas are prominently featured.
Jim Carrey and Téa Leoni's characters enjoy Margaritas during their suburban life.
The Tex-Mex border setting features Margaritas in the bar scenes.

name: Old Fashioned
preparation: Muddle sugar cube with bitters and water.

movieAppearances.note: George Bailey orders a series of Old Fashioneds at Martini's bar.
Ryan Gosling's character teaches Steve Carell to order an Old Fashioned instead of a vodka cranberry.
Officer K drinks an Old Fashioned at a bar while investigating.
Wall Street power players sip Old Fashioneds while the financial world crumbles around them.
Ben Affleck's meticulous character orders a precise Old Fashioned.
Don Draper makes the Old Fashioned his signature drink, even stepping behind the bar to mix one himself.

name: Negroni
preparation: Strain into a rocks glass over a large ice cube.

movieAppearances.note: Negronis are sipped in the Italian café scenes, fitting the film's Mediterranean setting.
Ryan Gosling's suave character introduces proper cocktails, including the Negroni.
Woody Allen's Roman comedy features Negronis as part of the Italian aperitivo tradition.

name: Daiquiri
preparation: Strain into a chilled coupe glass.

movieAppearances.note: Alec Guinness drinks Daiquiris in pre-revolution Havana, echoing Hemingway's famous habit.
A Daiquiri is ordered at a Havana bar in Michael Mann's stylish crime thriller.

name: Manhattan
preparation: Strain into a chilled coupe glass.

movieAppearances.note: Manhattans flow freely at the party on the train, made in a hot water bottle.
Burt Lancaster's J.J. Hunsecker drinks Manhattans in the jazz clubs of New York.
Jack Lemmon's character prepares Manhattans in his borrowed apartment.

name: Mojito
preparation: Gently muddle mint with syrup and lime juice.

movieAppearances.note: James Bond orders a Mojito in Havana, introducing the cocktail to a new generation.
Colin Farrell's Crockett drinks Mojitos in the sultry Miami nightlife scenes.
The Cuban classic appears alongside the Daiquiri in this Graham Greene adaptation.

name: Whiskey Sour
preparation: Dry shake all ingredients without ice.
Strain into a rocks glass over ice.

movieAppearances.note: Whiskey Sours are mixed and consumed during the memorable party scenes.
Humphrey Bogart's screenwriter character drinks Whiskey Sours at his local bar.
Ray Milland's alcoholic writer orders Whiskey Sours in Billy Wilder's harrowing drama.

movieAppearances.movie: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
movieAppearances.note: Leonardo DiCaprio's Rick Dalton confesses to drowning 'eight goddamn whiskey sours' the night before.

name: Martini
preparation: Strain into a chilled martini glass.

movieAppearances.note: Sean Connery's James Bond first orders a vodka martini, "shaken, not stirred."
Bond famously specifies his martini preferences while held captive.
Bette Davis holds court with a martini in the iconic party scenes.
Nick and Nora Charles make martini drinking look effortlessly glamorous throughout.
Martinis appear throughout as the detectives navigate 1950s Los Angeles.
Lorelai Gilmore steadies herself with a gin martini before the weekly Friday-night dinners with her parents.

name: Espresso Martini
preparation: Shake all ingredients vigorously with ice.
Strain into a chilled coupe glass.

movieAppearances.note: The Espresso Martini makes appearances as a trendy cocktail choice.

name: Cosmopolitan
preparation: Strain into a chilled coupe glass.

movieAppearances.note: Carrie Bradshaw and friends drink Cosmos throughout, continuing the TV series tradition.
The iconic foursome toast with Cosmopolitans in Abu Dhabi.
The Cosmo features as the stylish drink of choice at the local bar.
The HBO series turned the Cosmopolitan into a cultural phenomenon as Carrie and her friends drank them throughout its run.

name: Mai Tai
preparation: Strain into a rocks glass filled with crushed ice.

movieAppearances.note: Elvis Presley sips Mai Tais in this tropical musical, helping popularize tiki culture.
Mai Tais flow freely at the Hawaiian resort where Jason Segel's character tries to move on.
The characters discuss doing nothing and drinking Mai Tais as the ultimate fantasy.

name: Moscow Mule
preparation: Build in a copper mug over ice.

movieAppearances.note: Tony Stark's extravagant party lifestyle includes Moscow Mules in their signature copper mugs.

name: Aperol Spritz
preparation: Build in a wine glass over ice.

movieAppearances.note: Diane Lane's character embraces Italian life, with Aperol Spritzes as part of the aperitivo culture.
The Italian aperitivo tradition, including Spritzes, features in Woody Allen's Roman comedy.
The Venetian spritz is omnipresent in the film's Venice canal-side café scenes.

name: Piña Colada
preparation: Blend all ingredients with a cup of crushed ice until smooth.

movieAppearances.note: Tom Cruise blends Piña Coladas in his flashy bartending routines.
Tom Cruise's character enjoys Piña Coladas during his Cayman Islands trip.
The Hawaiian resort setting features Piña Coladas as the quintessential vacation drink.
Piña Coladas are served during the uncomfortable family gathering scenes.

name: Gin and Tonic
preparation: Build in a highball glass over ice.

movieAppearances.note: Katharine Hepburn's character disapproves of Humphrey Bogart's gin, though the spirit is central to the plot.
Robert Redford and Meryl Streep share gin and tonics on the Kenyan savannah.
The British retirees carry on the colonial tradition of G&Ts in India.
Gin and tonics flow at the aristocratic country estate.

name: Paloma
preparation: Build in a highball glass over ice.

movieAppearances.note: The Mexican border setting features the Paloma as the country's most popular tequila cocktail.

Arthur Ruskin (Alan King) holds forth on his love of the Sidecar while being interviewed by Bruce Willis's journalist.

name: Tom Collins
preparation: Shake gin, lemon juice and syrup with ice.

name: French 75

movieAppearances.note: Yvonne arrives at Rick's Café Américain with her new German-officer companion and they order French 75s — one of the few cocktails mixed on-screen.
Russell Crowe's character enjoys French 75s in the south of France.

name: Boulevardier
preparation: Strain into a rocks glass over a large ice cube.

movieAppearances.note: The 1920s Paris setting evokes the era when Erskine Gwynne, an American boulevardier, created this cocktail.

name: Gimlet
preparation: Strain into a chilled coupe glass.

name: Smoked Old Fashioned
preparation: Smoke a rocks glass with applewood chips. Place a small pile of applewood chips on a heat-safe surface or in a smoking device, ignite them with a kitchen torch until they smolder, then capture the smoke by inverting the glass over the chips for 30–60 seconds. Alternatively, use a handheld cocktail smoker clipped to the glass.
Add bourbon, syrup and bitters with a large ice cube and stir.

preparation: Shake Scotch, lemon and honey-ginger with ice.

name: Espresso Negroni
preparation: Strain into a rocks glass over a large ice cube.

name: Dark and Handsome
preparation: Strain into a rocks glass over a large ice cube.

name: El Diablo (Mezcal)
preparation: Shake mezcal, cassis and lime with ice.
Strain into a highball glass over ice.

name: Rum & Coke
preparation: Build in a highball glass over ice and stir.

movieAppearances.note: The simple rum and Coke is the drink of choice in the Havana nightlife scenes.
The fraternity party staple features prominently in the toga party scenes.

name: Gin Tonica
preparation: Build in a balloon wine glass over large ice cubes.

name: Suffering Bastard (Joe's)
preparation: Shake spirits, lime and bitters with ice.
Strain into a tiki mug over ice.

name: Daisy Buchanan
preparation: Shake gin, elderflower and lemon with ice.

movieAppearances.note: Named after F. Scott Fitzgerald's character, brought to life by Carey Mulligan in Baz Luhrmann's adaptation.
Mia Farrow's portrayal of Daisy in the Robert Redford version.

name: The Last Mechanical Art
preparation: Strain into a rocks glass over ice.

name: Vieux Mot
preparation: Strain into a chilled coupe glass.

name: The Word
preparation: Strain into a chilled coupe glass.

name: Greenpoint (Original)
preparation: Strain into a chilled coupe glass.

name: New York Sour (Classic)
preparation: Shake whiskey, lemon and syrup with ice.
Strain into a rocks glass over ice.
Carefully float red wine on top.

name: Night Cap
preparation: Strain into a chilled coupe glass.

name: Absinthe Frappé
preparation: Build absinthe and syrup over crushed ice in a rocks glass.

movieAppearances.note: Absinthe features prominently with the Green Fairy sequence in Baz Luhrmann's musical.
Johnny Depp's Inspector Abberline is an absinthe drinker in this Victorian-era thriller.
Absinthe is consumed in Coppola's Gothic horror, fitting the decadent Victorian era.
Jude Law's character enjoys absinthe cocktails in his London nightlife adventures.

name: Hemingway Gin & Tonic
preparation: Build in a highball glass over ice.

name: Breakfast Martini
preparation: Stir the marmalade with the lemon juice to dissolve.
Strain into a chilled coupe glass.

name: Gin Rickey
preparation: Build gin and lime juice over ice in a highball glass.

name: Spaghett
preparation: Sip or pour about 60 ml from a cold lager to make room.
Squeeze in the lemon wedges and drop them in.

name: Cynar Negroni
preparation: Strain into a rocks glass over a large ice cube.

name: Old Cuban
preparation: Lightly muddle the mint with the lime juice and sugar syrup.
Add the rum and bitters, then shake hard with ice.

name: Kentucky Buck
preparation: Muddle the strawberry in the shaker.
Add the bourbon, lemon juice, sugar syrup and bitters, then shake with ice.
Strain into an ice-filled highball glass.

name: Cobble Hill
preparation: Muddle the cucumber in a mixing glass.
Add the remaining ingredients and stir with ice.

name: Conference
preparation: Strain into a rocks glass over a large ice cube.

name: Mexican 55
preparation: Shake the tequila, lime juice and agave syrup with ice.

name: Saoco
preparation: Build all ingredients in a highball glass over ice.
